metadata.name: Wonderscapes #237
metadata.description:
In the infinite abyss of wondering there are some random moments of beauty which will stay in memory and at the end they will remind us that exploration and efforts were not in vain.

This artwork is rendered in real-time using custom webStyleGAN model (~25mb) which was trained on a dataset of about 5000 wonderscapes generated by DALL-E from OpenAI.

In addition to the main generative model, there is a CNN classifier model that filters the rendered images by predicting similarity to landscapes (this may take some time with each generation).

On top of the image generated by the neural network, there is a processing script that converts the value of each pixel into a colored ASCII character, the final result is a 64x64 vector grid with 4096 text symbols.

Controls:
- Press [s] to save the high resolution image

metadata.name: Inland Emotions #91
metadata.description:
Inland Emotions

Over the centuries, ancient philosophers and modern psychologists have come to the same basic conclusion. The fact is that, according to a few millennia of research, there are a limited number of distinct human emotions, predefined by the human psyche. Sometimes it may seem like we are experiencing a "new" one, but on closer inspection, we see that it is the known emotions that are superimposed. Emotions are what they are, and they exist in the same way in each of us, whether we recognize them for what they are or not. But as our history blends into a new emerging state of the world, a whole new range of emotional responses are triggered within us. An infinite maelstrom of variations generated from a simple combination of a few emotions.


Controls :

• Press 1, 2, 3, or 4 to make a capture and download a PNG with a resolution of x1, x2, x3 or x4.

———————

Inland Emotions by Ferdinand Dervieux, 2022
Made with Unity
License: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0

metadata.name: Ambient Landscapes synthesizer #25
metadata.description:
Ambient Landscapes is an audiovisual modular synthesizer.

Each edition has unique base sequence, scale, parameter combination, and visual landscape.

The generation principle is based on aliasing technique - a base sequence is modulated to hearable range, then passed through an effect chain and demodulated back to a sequence. Passed through quantizer this gives an almost infinite continuous field of base melody variations, completely controllable by parameters of the effect chain. Besides that, one also can control pitch, tempo, noise amount, filters and shape of the second melody by dragging the stars in the constellations. Each star can also be toggled to drift and evolve in automatic mode.

Interface:
- click / space to start
- mouse to drag dots and change params
- "e" while dragging to toggle dot auto evolution
- "v" to unfold to full screen (also gives better performance)
- "r" to reset dots to initial positions

Engine: @ferluht
Generative patch design: @alexeev
January 2022

metadata.name: Backyard #99
metadata.description:
The house I lived in when I was young was a house with a small backyard with brick walls. I often imagined it in the backyard at the time.
If I grow up and have my own house later, I want to live in a house with a backyard with a dense nature and a view of a vast mountain.
Although I haven't achieved the imagination that I did when I was young yet. I'm so happy because I think it came true in the world of generative art.
I hope you have the backyard that you imagine.

--

Features:
 - Canvas: vertical / horizontal
 - Palette: black / red / green / purple / yellow / blue
 - Highlighted object: tree / flowers / brickwall
 - MountainType: standard / sharp
 - Tree: true / false
 - Flowers: true / false
 - Stars: true / false
 - Fog: true / false
 - Brickwall: true / false
 - Sun or Moon: true / false / supersize
 - After the rain: true / false

--

Press "s" in live mode to download the rendered image as a png file. (2000 x 3000 for vertical, 3000 x 1500 for horizontal)

Made with p5js and passion
By thion, September 2023

metadata.name: Intermezzo #50
metadata.description:
The title Intermezzo is derived from the musical term and also the title of the famous work by Ukrainian writer Mykhailo Kotsiubynsky. In music, an intermezzo, in the most general sense, is a composition which fits between other musical or dramatic entities, such as acts of a play or movements of a larger musical work. The project draws inspiration from Kotsiubynsky's Intermezzo, which reflects the writer's personal experiences with escaping the city and finding solace in nature during times of hardship in Ukraine in the early 1900s. For the lyrical hero of Kotsyubynskyi's novel, such a musical break is a spiritual rebirth of man in nature. In the context of contemporary Ukraine, Intermezzo represents an escape from the daily news cycle, despair,and death that the country is currently experiencing due to ongoing war. 
The project is a search for peace, balance, and harmony in the midst of turmoil. By combining zoomorphic organic sculptures Intermezzo creates an experience that invites the audience to reflect on their own personal relationships with nature and the need for escapism in times of hardship.

metadata.name: aether:Astral Loom #165
metadata.description:
Weaving on the astral plane. Lines of transmission. 
Some journeys are straightforward, others yield a multitude of pathways.
Disconnect to interpret.

(As an experiment) the rarity values assigned to this artwork are completely arbitrary, and are in no way connected to the visual output of the minted piece. One might wonder, why have rarity values at all? That's what I ask myself any time I see a value based on rarity attributes instead of viewer aesthetic. 

That being said, I suppose it's up to each person to decide for themselves the value of art...

This artwork was created using p5js.

linktr.ee/lisaorthstudio
twitter: @lisaorthstudio

metadata.name: DOS DELIGHT: DITHER #62
metadata.description:
As a child who encountered computers in the nostalgic era of the 90s, 'Dos Delight' stands as one of my most intimate generative art projects, bridging the gap between my early childhood and vivid memories intertwined with the world of computing.

In this generative art, I aim to recreate the aesthetics of 90s computers, tapping into the nostalgia of a bygone era. The algorithm, inspired by the playfulness of childhood, generates whimsical, childlike paintings reminiscent of a time when computers were new, and learning happened through wild exploration.

Crafted to capture the boundless creativity of a child lost in play, the algorithm yields artworks adorned with pixelated allure, employing a captivating blend of black and white enhanced by dither aesthetics.

Press 4 or 8 to export in 4k and 8k in PNG.
Press 's' to export in screen resolution.

Murat Atimtay 2024 | @atimtay | https://www.atimtay.xyz"

metadata.name: Slumbers #45
metadata.description:
Generative abstract artwork that explores forms, shapes, coloring and brushwork.

Options:

- press 'f' to turn off paper-like texture (recommended for print on hi-quality paper)
- press 's' to save the PNG image of the current view
- press 1, 2, 3, or 4 to save hi-res PNG images

Colors and textures might differ slightly on some devices due to the heavy use of low alpha blending. Calibrated and tested on Firefox 107.0 & Chrome 107.0.5304.107 on Win10 x64 Intel machine.

Created using p5js.

@VolatileMoods, November, 2022.
tags: slumbers volatile moods abstract generative painting colors brushwork
